llm.response_raw: {"ecutwfc_Ry": 60.0, "ecutrho_Ry": 480.0, "kpoints": [6, 6, 6], "smearing": "gaussian", "degauss_Ry": 0.005, "conv_thr_Ry": 1e-8, "mixing_beta": 0.4, "reasoning": "Pb 6s2/6p2 + 5d10 semicore and Cs 5s/5p valence demand higher ecut than Si (60 Ry chosen, conservative). Halide I is compact 5s5p so doesn't dominate. ecutrho 8x=480 Ry to resolve Pb d-density. Cubic 5-atom cell at a=6.29 Å can use modest k=6x6x6; Pm-3m symmetry folds to ~10 IBZ so cheap. Insulating halide perovskite => gaussian smearing + tight degauss. mixing_beta=0.4 because heavy Pb + halide tends to charge-slosh."}
